"opinion": "The gender gap in science is a pressing issue that needs to be addressed. As a 70-year-old male from Lecce, Italy, with a background in waitering/waitressing and Christianity, I believe that increasing diversity and inclusion in STEM fields can help bridge this gap. This can be achieved by providing more opportunities for girls and women to pursue higher education and careers in these fields, as well as addressing systemic barriers and biases that prevent them from doing so. Additionally, promoting a culture of inclusivity and respect can help create a more welcoming environment for everyone in STEM, regardless of gender.
